title: VPN for Firestick and Fire TV: Native Apps, Sideloading, and SmartDNS
description: How to install a VPN on Amazon Firestick and Fire TV in 2026. Covers native Appstore apps, sideloading via Downloader, Vega OS restrictions, and SmartDNS as an alternative.
content:
## Why You Need a VPN on Firestick

Amazon's Fire TV platform is the most popular streaming stick in the world, but it ships with significant restrictions. Your ISP can see exactly what you stream and can throttle your connection when it detects high video traffic. A VPN encrypts that traffic so your ISP sees nothing useful, and it lets you access streaming libraries from other countries.

The other reason people run VPNs on Firestick is IPTV. Many third-party IPTV services operate in a legal grey area, and streaming them without a VPN exposes your IP address to the content owners scanning those streams.

## Which VPN Apps Are Available Natively on the Amazon Appstore

Several major VPNs publish dedicated Fire TV apps directly to the Amazon Appstore. You can install these exactly like any other app, no extra steps required:

- **NordVPN** has a native Fire TV app with a dedicated streaming-optimized interface. NordLynx (its WireGuard implementation) consistently delivers the fastest speeds on Firestick hardware.
- **Surfshark** has a Fire TV app and supports unlimited simultaneous connections, which matters if you run multiple streaming devices.
- **IPVanish** has been available on the Appstore for years and is a popular choice for IPTV users because it has no logs and fast US servers.
- **ExpressVPN** has a Fire TV app with a simple one-button interface, though it is the most expensive option.
- **Proton VPN** offers a free tier with unlimited data, which is the only genuinely useful free option on Firestick.

To install any of these: open the Appstore on your Fire TV, search by name, and install. No settings changes needed.

## Sideloading a VPN via the Downloader App

If the VPN you want does not have a native Appstore app, you can sideload the Android APK. The process:

1. Install the **Downloader** app from the Appstore (it is free).
2. Go to Settings > My Fire TV > Developer Options and enable **Apps from Unknown Sources**.
3. Open Downloader and enter the direct APK download URL from your VPN provider.
4. Install the downloaded APK and launch it.

Most VPN providers publish a direct APK link in their help centre. NordVPN, Surfshark, and Private Internet Access all do.

## The Vega OS Problem

Amazon's newer Fire TV hardware runs **Vega OS**, which completely blocks sideloading. If you have one of these devices, the Downloader sideload method will fail:

- Fire TV Stick 4K Select (2025 release)
- Fire TV Stick HD (2026 release)

On Vega OS devices, you can only install apps from the Appstore. If your VPN does not have an Appstore listing, you have two options: switch to a VPN that does (NordVPN and Surfshark both do), or use SmartDNS instead.

Older devices (Fire TV Stick 4K Plus, Fire TV Stick 4K Max, original Fire TV Stick 4K) still run the Android-based Fire OS and support sideloading.

## SmartDNS as an Alternative

SmartDNS does not encrypt your traffic, but it reroutes your DNS queries through a server in another country. This is enough to unlock geo-restricted streaming services without any speed loss, because there is no VPN tunnel overhead.

NordVPN and Surfshark both include SmartDNS with every subscription. To set it up on a Vega OS Firestick:

1. Log into your VPN account on a computer and find the SmartDNS IP addresses in the account settings.
2. On your Fire TV: Settings > Network > (your Wi-Fi network) > Advanced > DNS > enter the SmartDNS IP.
3. Reboot the Fire TV.

SmartDNS works for unblocking Netflix US, BBC iPlayer, Disney+, and similar services. It does not protect your privacy or stop ISP throttling.

## Buffering and Speed Tips

A VPN always adds some overhead. To keep buffering to a minimum on Firestick:

- Connect to a server geographically close to you unless you specifically need another country.
- Use WireGuard or NordLynx protocol rather than OpenVPN. WireGuard is significantly faster on Firestick's limited CPU.
- Avoid free VPNs. Their servers are overloaded and the bandwidth per user is too low for video streaming.

## Recommendation

**NordVPN** is the best all-round choice for Firestick. It has a native Appstore app, supports both Vega OS and older Fire OS devices, includes SmartDNS, and NordLynx keeps speeds high enough for 4K. If you want something cheaper and need unlimited simultaneous connections across all your devices, **Surfshark** does the same job for less money.
